characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Abusive Offspring: Gus and Wally often mistreat YAY-OK, not giving him the credit nor respect that one who saves their lives or cleans up after them constantly would deserve. Barefoot Cartoon Animal: Even though they wear full spacesuits, neither of them wear shoes. Big Brother Worship: Baby YAY is not only one of the few characters who are hardly rude or snarky to Gus and Wally, he also looks up to them more than his own father YAY-OK. Bite of Affection: Some episodes imply they chew on Lord Peel because they like him. YAY-OK reveals sometimes they chew on him. Depending on the Writer:Whether or not they are completely selfish or have good qualities deep down depends on the episode.Same with if they are capable of being heroic or are completely helpless.

characters.name: Gus
characters.description: Abhorrent Admirer:He has a huge crush on Monkevil, but she's shown to absolutely despise him in "Say It Ain't Bro" which is the last episode she appears in. She slaps him and calls him rancid for giving her a box of poop instead of escape tools.He gets his own in the form of Jo-Jo, a two headed alien who stalks him. He has a huge crush on Monkevil, but she's shown to absolutely despise him in "Say It Ain't Bro" which is the last episode she appears in. She slaps him and calls him rancid for giving her a box of poop instead of escape tools. He gets his own in the form of Jo-Jo, a two headed alien who stalks him. Adaptation Personality Change: In the 2008 pilot, he's serious and grumpy, but in the series proper, he is more goofy and hyper. Apologizes a Lot: Only in Season 1, he often ends the episode saying "I'm sorry." to Wally. Ironically though in "So Sorry" he doesn't even know the concept of an apology.

characters.name: Wally
characters.description: Annoying Younger Sibling: He frequently causes problems for Gus. Big Eater: He's seen snacking on things (especially if it's bananas) more out of the two, which explains why he often gets moreBalloon Bellyshots than his bro does. Break the Cutie: Any instance where he cries for whatever reason can be seen as this, but the worst example is in "Say It Ain't Bro" where he becomes so upset about Gus disowning him (for...a mop) that he has a raincloud over his head. Cloudcuckoolander: He generally saysmuchweirder things than Gus and most of the time has no idea what's going on. It comes with being the less sensible one of the two. Cute and Psycho: An "adorable" monkey who is often immature, disgusting and obnoxious, and (along with his brother Gus) frequently showcases aLack of Empathytowards YAY-OK.

characters.name: YAY-OK
characters.description: Adaptational Intelligence: In the 2008 pilot he was well meaning but clumsy and clueless. In the final version he's the only one who gets things done around the monkeys' ship. Affectionate Nickname: The monkeys would occasionally refer to him as "Bro-bot". Beleaguered Assistant: He usually is the one who saves the day in the end, doing just about everything the monkeys are assigned to do, while also constantly having to deal with the monkeys' stupidity. Big Brother Instinct: He is very protective of his younger sister Hooray-OK, even threatening Gus and Wally so they won't interact with her. Break the Cutie: The poor guy is always reduced to a sobbing wreck in certain situations where he thinks he will be permanently seperated from his monkeys.

characters.name: Dr. Chimpsky
characters.description: A Day in the Limelight: While he's an important character, he only has one episode mainly focused on him "The Doctor Is Out", where he is demoted to the bottom and has to prove himself to save Gus and Wally and bring peace back to the galaxy. Deliberately Monochrome: He lives inside of a black and white screen, he's usually never seen in color except for in "The Doctor Is Out" after he's been demoted, and in some other episodes such as "I'm YAY-OK You're Not YAY-OK", "Monkeys vs. Gorillas" and "The Chimpsky Dimension". Expy: Him being an older man with aDeliberately Monochromestyle inside a monitor makes him similar to Old Rabbit Ears fromThe Brave Little Toaster, though he’s an actual human as opposed to atalking TVwith a human avatar. Fan Disservice: He's often seen shirtless wearing outfits such as bikinis and bras and is implied to be naked offscreen. He is generally very hairy with a beer belly. Heroic BSoD: After being demoted to a Junior Junior Junior Rocket Monkey Third Class (one rank below Gus and Wally), he breaks down in tears then tries to be a cuckoo clock salesman who makes clocks of him telling himself he's a failure. YAY-OK has to convince him to step up and save the monkeys.

characters.name: Inky
characters.description: All Animals Are Dogs: He is an octopus-like alien, but he fetches like a dog would. Black Comedy Animal Cruelty: In "The Long Goodbye", Gus and Wally are so distraught over Inky leaving, that they cope by replacing him with a puppy, hanging up crossed out images of Inky, and even letting the puppy attack him. Butt-Monkey: Ironically because of the monkeys. They are rather careless with him, putting him in danger without noticing. Expy: Zigzagged. While Season 1 came out before "The Penguins of Madagascar'', the episode "The Long Goodbye" came out after. In it he seems to be an expy of Dave, both are purple villainous cephalopods who are upset about being neglected due to new supposedly cuter animals, so they try to get revenge on said critters. Furry Confusion: Is a pet cephalopod whilst Nefarious (an octopus-like alien) is a well known villain who's just seen as another person.

characters.name: Bernice
characters.description: I Am Not Your Father: In "The Last Peel" it's revealed she's just an actress hired to play as the monkeys' mother. She finally has enough and shouts "I'M NOT YOUR MOTHER!!!" I Have No Son!: If she feels betrayed by Gus and Wally, she will not hesitate to threaten disowning them, but she quickly changes her mind. In "Mom Loves Me More" she says they would be dead to her just for tricking her Inexplicably Tailless: Despite being called a monkey and not an ape, she has no tail unlike Gus and Wally. Kavorka Man: She is a large overweight monkey with a large nose and a high pitched man's voice, not the most conventionally attractive primate, but she goes through many boyfriends, most of which adore her in return. My Beloved Smother: She is overprotective of Gus and Wally, in "Bro-Bot Knows Best" she censors a movie so much, it's shortened to a few seconds.

characters.name: Lord Peel
characters.description: Ambiguously Gay: In "I am a Banana" he has the banana equivalent to a pride parade when he pretends to embrace his identity as a banana. Plus he seems to have a thing for YAY-OK.In "Robo-Butler" they become friends and Peel desperate to keep YAY-OK around. He even gives him a box with a medal, said box being shaped like a bigger engagement ring box.In another episode, he ends up having biological children with YAY-OK. In "Robo-Butler" they become friends and Peel desperate to keep YAY-OK around. He even gives him a box with a medal, said box being shaped like a bigger engagement ring box. In another episode, he ends up having biological children with YAY-OK. Anthropomorphic Food:Subverted; he is just a man who looks like a banana,and is not actually a living banana. That doesn't stop the Rocket Monkeys from constantly mistaking him for one. Ax-Crazy: He always comes up with schemes to get revenge on Gus and Wally, most involving them being killed brutally.

characters.name: Nefarious
characters.description: Ambiguously Christian: Not only does he celebrate Christmas like most of the other characters, he even sets up a rock formation of the Nativity. Bald of Evil: He has a balding hairline and is a villain Beard of Evil: Has a mustache and a goatee. Birthday Hater: Like Gus, he hates his own birthday, the two even bond over it despite being enemies. Card-Carrying Villain: What else to expect from someone who calls himselfNefarious?

characters.name: Slo-Mo
characters.description: Abduction Is Love: She kidnaps YAY-OK in "Monkey's Best Friend" while Nefarious is out trying to trick the monkeys. Deadpan Snarker: Often snarks at her boss/creator. The Dragon: Is this to Nefarious, being his robot assistant. Even Evil Has Loved Ones: She genuinely seems to care for YAY-OK, despite him being on the opposing side. She is also kind to Inky, more so than Gus and Wally usually are. Fembot: She is a female robot, with bolts serving as pigtails.

characters.name: Monkevil
characters.description: Abandoned Catchphrase: In her Season 3 appearances, she starts using "Poopy doopy"less. In "So Mature", she only says it three times despite the large amount of screentime she had, and in "Say It Ain't Bro", she only says itonce. Action Girl: In "Monkey Hearts" when she temporarily turns good and joins Gus and Wally's crew, she proves to be stronger and smarter than both of them. She gets through a dangerous obstacle course with ease and is able to learn the controls of the ship quickly. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: Played with in "Always a Monkey, Never a Bride", after she saves Gus and Wally, Monkevil responds to the monkeys asking if she likes them by saying she does even though they have a list of negative qualities. But as they are arguing over her, she ditches them and steals Wally's wedding gifts. Beauty Is Never Tarnished: She's a girl monkey and never willingly performs any disgusting moments onscreen, compared to the other characters that do so, who are mostly male. The Cameo: Has non-speaking ones in "Golden Nugglets" and "The Button". She also appears as an opponent in the gameMonkeys on a Mission.

characters.name: Cornelius
characters.description: Embarrassing Nickname: Gus and Wally only call him by "Corny" and many other nicknames he hates such as "Corn on the Cob". I Can't Believe It's Not Heroin!: Whenever the monkeys force him to eat a banana, he begins to act just about as crazy as they do. Success as Revenge: He swears revenge on the Rocket Monkeys for getting him fired from GASI. But the next time he's seen he is running a successful restaurant and locks Gus and Wally out. We Used to Be Friends: He was Gus's old friend back in school, but due to the chaos they caused with Wally, he decided to move on from them. After they get him fired from his lab, he absolutely hates them.

characters.name: Dadbot
characters.description: Abusive Parents: He hates his son YAY-OK for being too weak and having no drive to destroy. All There in the Manual: Concept art reveals his actual name to be "NO-OK", which is never said in the show. Fartillery: His farts can set others on fire and deform their faces as he demonstrated towards Gus and Wally. Gruesome Grandparent: He farts toxic fire at Gus and Wally, who's more or less his son's adopted kids, for daring to stand up to him. He also threatened to kill them for almost getting Hooray-OK hurt. Like Father, Unlike Son: He is a Doombot who wants to destroy anything that moves whilst YAY-OK is a friendly robot who cleans up messes (mostly made by his monkeys).
